channel.title: Roberts Radio Briefings
channel.description: Daily briefings and deep-dives from Roberts Radio.
channel.itunes:author: Roberts Radio

item.title: Carbon's Accidental Architecture — and the Battery Arms Race Reshaping Every Grid From Chile to New South Wales
item.description: Today's briefing takes the long view on carbon markets, tracing how a post-communist soap factory became the unlikely godfather of the voluntary credits your clients are buying right now. We also cover a remarkable week in battery storage, from a 700 Wh/kg chemistry breakthrough to a 2.6 GWh Chilean deal and a fresh 16 GWh Italian auction that will matter enormously for your Iberian modelling peers next door.
item.itunes:title: Carbon's Accidental Architecture — and the Battery Arms Race Reshaping Every Grid From Chile to New South Wales
item.itunes:summary: Today's briefing takes the long view on carbon markets, tracing how a post-communist soap factory became the unlikely godfather of the voluntary credits your clients are buying right now. We also cover a remarkable week in battery storage, from a 700 Wh/kg chemistry breakthrough to a 2.6 GWh Chilean deal and a fresh 16 GWh Italian auction that will matter enormously for your Iberian modelling peers next door.

item.title: The post-handover blind spot: how UK building regs are letting developers off the carbon hook
item.description: Today's deep-dive unpacks the quiet loophole in the UK's latest building performance regulations that lets developers walk away from operational carbon responsibility the moment they hand over the keys. We also catch up on a busy weekend in energy storage, offshore wind, and Iberian market moves that your clients will want to know about.
item.itunes:title: The post-handover blind spot: how UK building regs are letting developers off the carbon hook
item.itunes:summary: Today's deep-dive unpacks the quiet loophole in the UK's latest building performance regulations that lets developers walk away from operational carbon responsibility the moment they hand over the keys. We also catch up on a busy weekend in energy storage, offshore wind, and Iberian market moves that your clients will want to know about.

item.title: Wind records, building rules and the long road to bankable storage
item.description: Britain breaks a wind generation record on the same weekend the government finally confirms its Future Homes and Buildings Standards, while the long-duration storage sector wrestles with how to move from promising chemistry to credible project finance. A busy Monday morning in the energy transition.
item.itunes:title: Wind records, building rules and the long road to bankable storage
item.itunes:summary: Britain breaks a wind generation record on the same weekend the government finally confirms its Future Homes and Buildings Standards, while the long-duration storage sector wrestles with how to move from promising chemistry to credible project finance. A busy Monday morning in the energy transition.

item.title: The Carbon We Can't See: How Embodied Emissions Are Reshaping the Built Environment
item.description: The carbon released before a building ever switches on a light is fast becoming the defining challenge of climate policy in the built environment. From concrete's inconvenient chemistry to a growing regulatory push on whole-life carbon, Sunday's briefing explores why the demolish-or-retrofit question is no longer just an architectural one — it's a carbon accounting crisis with real consequences for your clients' project pipelines.
item.itunes:title: The Carbon We Can't See: How Embodied Emissions Are Reshaping the Built Environment
item.itunes:summary: The carbon released before a building ever switches on a light is fast becoming the defining challenge of climate policy in the built environment. From concrete's inconvenient chemistry to a growing regulatory push on whole-life carbon, Sunday's briefing explores why the demolish-or-retrofit question is no longer just an architectural one — it's a carbon accounting crisis with real consequences for your clients' project pipelines.

item.title: Saturday Dispatch: Ancient Scripts, Crumbling Institutions, and the Price of Everything
item.description: This morning we take a detour through Neal Stephenson's Snow Crash and what cuneiform can teach us about language, power, and the architecture of reality. Along the way: NASA's space station mess, Apple's quiet retreat from the Mac Pro, and why AV1's royalty-free promise may be less solid than Silicon Valley would like you to believe.
item.itunes:title: Saturday Dispatch: Ancient Scripts, Crumbling Institutions, and the Price of Everything
item.itunes:summary: This morning we take a detour through Neal Stephenson's Snow Crash and what cuneiform can teach us about language, power, and the architecture of reality. Along the way: NASA's space station mess, Apple's quiet retreat from the Mac Pro, and why AV1's royalty-free promise may be less solid than Silicon Valley would like you to believe.

item.title: Wind records, battery booms, and the quiet rewiring of the energy system
item.description: Britain breaks its wind generation record and hits 22 GW of solar, while a global surge in battery storage investment reshapes everything from AI data centres to community energy. Meanwhile, the frameworks underpinning how we measure and price carbon risk are quietly being rebuilt from the ground up.
item.itunes:title: Wind records, battery booms, and the quiet rewiring of the energy system
item.itunes:summary: Britain breaks its wind generation record and hits 22 GW of solar, while a global surge in battery storage investment reshapes everything from AI data centres to community energy. Meanwhile, the frameworks underpinning how we measure and price carbon risk are quietly being rebuilt from the ground up.

item.title: Pension Capital, Fiduciary Duty, and the Quiet Rewiring of Britain's Energy Future
item.description: From landmark CfD contracts to Chinese turbine bans and a Scottish nacelle factory, today's news is thick with signals about who is building the UK's clean energy infrastructure — and who is paying for it. Beneath the headlines, a deeper story is emerging about how institutional capital is reshaping what gets built, where, and on whose terms.
item.itunes:title: Pension Capital, Fiduciary Duty, and the Quiet Rewiring of Britain's Energy Future
item.itunes:summary: From landmark CfD contracts to Chinese turbine bans and a Scottish nacelle factory, today's news is thick with signals about who is building the UK's clean energy infrastructure — and who is paying for it. Beneath the headlines, a deeper story is emerging about how institutional capital is reshaping what gets built, where, and on whose terms.

item.title: Battery Billions, Vestas in Scotland, and the Quiet Reshaping of Buildings Policy
item.description: A bumper Thursday for energy storage deals and clean energy manufacturing, with Brookfield snapping up Canada's biggest BESS developer and Vestas confirming a Scottish nacelle factory. Beneath the deal-flow, the policy architecture around buildings decarbonisation and electricity market design is quietly being redrawn on both sides of the Channel.
item.itunes:title: Battery Billions, Vestas in Scotland, and the Quiet Reshaping of Buildings Policy
item.itunes:summary: A bumper Thursday for energy storage deals and clean energy manufacturing, with Brookfield snapping up Canada's biggest BESS developer and Vestas confirming a Scottish nacelle factory. Beneath the deal-flow, the policy architecture around buildings decarbonisation and electricity market design is quietly being redrawn on both sides of the Channel.

item.title: The Quiet Rewiring of Britain: How Heat Pumps Are Reshaping the Grid From the Inside Out
item.description: Mass heat pump adoption isn't just a story about boilers and installers — it's forcing a fundamental rethink of how the UK's electricity network is planned, priced, and operated. The decisions being made right now, in 2026, will lock in either a virtuous cycle of falling costs and smart flexibility, or a vicious one of socialised grid upgrades and consumer backlash. Here's what's at stake.
item.itunes:title: The Quiet Rewiring of Britain: How Heat Pumps Are Reshaping the Grid From the Inside Out
item.itunes:summary: Mass heat pump adoption isn't just a story about boilers and installers — it's forcing a fundamental rethink of how the UK's electricity network is planned, priced, and operated. The decisions being made right now, in 2026, will lock in either a virtuous cycle of falling costs and smart flexibility, or a vicious one of socialised grid upgrades and consumer backlash. Here's what's at stake.

item.title: Storage Moves Centre Stage: From French Subsidies to AI Data Centres
item.description: Battery storage is asserting itself across every corner of the energy transition this week — reshaping subsidy design in France, powering AI ambitions in California, and setting new records in the US. Meanwhile, the UK quietly mandates heat pumps and solar for new homes, and perovskite technology edges closer to commercial reality in China.
item.itunes:title: Storage Moves Centre Stage: From French Subsidies to AI Data Centres
item.itunes:summary: Battery storage is asserting itself across every corner of the energy transition this week — reshaping subsidy design in France, powering AI ambitions in California, and setting new records in the US. Meanwhile, the UK quietly mandates heat pumps and solar for new homes, and perovskite technology edges closer to commercial reality in China.
